clinical_history: Young adult male with palpitations and syncope.  The diagnosis is:
diagnosis: e) The classic triad of Wolff-Parkinson-White (WPW) is present: short PR, wide QRS and delta waves.  The delta waves are negative in III and aVF, and positive in V1-V3 which is consistent with a left posterior bypass tract.  This pattern simulates inferior-posterior myocardial infarction (MI).  WPW may also simulate left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH) or right ventricular hypertrophy (RVH,) as well as bundle branch blocks.  Duchenne's muscular dystrophy may be associated with a pattern resembling posterior MI. This patient had a cardiac electrophysiology (EP) study.  He was found to have a left posterior bypass tract (as well as a concealed left lateral bypass tract).  He had inducible atrioventricular reentrant tachycardia (AVRT) and atrial fibrillation.  Both bypass tracts were successfully ablated.
